title: Heaven as a State of the Soul — Swedenborg and Spiritual Space
author: Emanuel Swedenborg

body.text: Swedenborg gives one of the clearest pre-Corbin descriptions of visionary geography. Heaven has societies, directions, houses, garments, speech, and distances. These correspond to inner states. Space in heaven is measured by love, wisdom, and affinity — miles.
XVII Changes of State of the Angels in Heaven. XXII Space in Heaven.

The sequence matters. Swedenborg places space after state and time. Spiritual geography cannot be understood by location first. Understand the life of angels — love, wisdom, interior form — then space appears as the outer form of state. A society of love appears as a dwelling. Wisdom appears as light. Difference appears as distance.

body.caption: The heavenly city — a city of states, not of measurable distances.

body.text: By changes of state of angels, these alternations following one after another with variety.

Two souls side by side in physical space may be worlds apart in spiritual space. Two souls separated by oceans may be neighbours. The inner becomes spatial: this is Swedenborg's precise imaginal rule. Place is primary — state is primary. Space appears as the visible shape of what the soul has become.

body.caption: Affinity as geography — distance measured by love, not miles.

body.text: In the spiritual world, all changes of place are changes of state of the interiors.

This is why Corbin recognised Swedenborg as a Western witness — the same law governs Na-koja-Abad and Swedenborg's heaven. Geography is the soul's interior made visible. The imaginal world has stable structures: the same inner states produce the same landscapes across traditions. Affinity replaces distance. State replaces location.
Heaven is everywhere and nowhere. It is not a place you go. It is what you become capable of perceiving when the inner state is ripe for it.
